pattern: Doubling a big-endian digit list is the same ripple-carry arithmetic as adding a number to itself, just walked from the least significant digit backward.
hints: The head is the most significant digit, so any new digit produced by a carry out of the very first node has to become a brand new head.
Work from the last node toward the first: double a digit, add whatever carry the node to its right produced, and keep the overflow for the node to its left.

Collect the nodes into a list first so you can walk them right-to-left; after updating every node.val in place, if a carry of 1 is still left over, prepend one new ListNode(1) in front of the original head.

edge_inputs.description: single digit that carries out to two digits
doubling produces no new leading digit
a zero digit sits in the middle of the number
every digit is nine, forcing a brand new leading digit
largest possible digit value repeated, longer run
carry propagates through several nines then stops before the head

stress.description: 10000-digit worst case, all nines, to exercise a full carry ripple and a new head
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    digits = [9] * n
    return [digits]
stress.note: forces carry to propagate across every node and prepend a new head digit

stress.description: 10000 random digits with a non-zero leading digit
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    if n == 1:
        return [[rng.randrange(0, 10)]]
    first = rng.randrange(1, 10)
    rest = [rng.randrange(0, 10) for _ in range(n - 1)]
    return [[first] + rest]

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def doubleIt(self, head):
        digits = []
        node = head
        while node is not None:
            digits.append(str(node.val))
            node = node.next
        value = int(''.join(digits)) * 2
        result_digits = str(value)
        new_head = ListNode(int(result_digits[0]))
        cur = new_head
        for ch in result_digits[1:]:
            cur.next = ListNode(int(ch))
            cur = cur.next
        return new_head

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def doubleIt(self, head):
        nodes = []
        node = head
        while node is not None:
            nodes.append(node)
            node = node.next
        carry = 0
        for n in reversed(nodes):
            total = n.val * 2 + carry
            n.val = total % 10
            carry = total // 10
        if carry:
            new_head = ListNode(carry)
            new_head.next = head
            return new_head
        return head
